$comment: Single source of truth for the Agent Identity lifecycle controlled vocabulary. All terms are grounded in 'Identity Management for Agentic AI' (OpenID Foundation, Tobin South, October 2025). The repo-level validator (scripts/validate_lifecycle.py) enforces that surreal/*.surql uses only this vocabulary and that the state grammar matches the schema. No term may be introduced from outside the source document.

additionalSources.title: Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) v1.1
additionalSources.usedFor: Exact DID document property names (§5 Core Properties). The concept of DIDs is grounded in the OpenID whitepaper §3.1 'Sovereign and Portable Agent Identity'.

additionalSources.title: Identity Security Cloud API v3
additionalSources.usedFor: Knowledge-graph governance nodes/edges (sources, accounts, entitlements, access profiles, roles). The IGA concept is grounded in the OpenID whitepaper §2.12 and §2.9 SCIM.

additionalSources.title: Management Schema API
additionalSources.usedFor: Governed attribute definition fields (type, required, mutability, permissions, master) on the knowledge-graph schema_attribute node.

additionalSources.title: CyberArk API
additionalSources.usedFor: Privileged Access Management nodes: Safes that hold privileged accounts/credentials (knowledge-graph safe node and stored_in edge).

graph.$comment: Graph-layer edges connecting identity/lifecycle/DID nodes. Every edge must be a RELATION table in the schema, a key in the JSON-LD context, and in the glossary.

edges.definition: One agent delegates a subset of its authority to another agent (a delegation chain).
edges.source: OpenID whitepaper Key Terms: Recursive Delegation, Scope Attenuation; §3.2

edges.definition: An agent acts for a human principal while staying distinct from them.
edges.source: OpenID whitepaper Key Terms: On-Behalf-Of (OBO) Flow, Delegated Authority; §3.2

edges.definition: An agent operates within a trust domain.
edges.source: OpenID whitepaper Key Terms: Trust Domain

edges.definition: An agent is linked to each of its lifecycle audit-log entries.
A DID document links to its verification methods (keys).
A DID document links to its service endpoints.

knowledgeGraph.$comment: Governance knowledge graph promoted from SailPoint IGA + Okta Schema. Every node/edge must appear in surreal/schema/agent_knowledge_graph.surql.

nodes.definition: A connected system where accounts live.
A login record on a source.
The finest-grained unit of access on a source.
A bundle of entitlements granted together.
A bundle of access profiles for a job function.
A container that holds privileged accounts/credentials.

edges.definition: An agent identity holds an account.
An account lives on a source.
A privileged account is stored in a safe.
An entitlement is defined on a source.
An agent is assigned an access profile.
An agent is assigned a role.
An agent has a governed attribute.

did.definition: The entity that controls the DID document.
Other identifiers for the same subject.
Cryptographic material used to authenticate or authorize.
Verification relationship for proving control of the DID.
Verification relationship for issuing verifiable claims.
Verification relationship for establishing shared keys.
Verification relationship for invoking a capability.
Verification relationship for delegating a capability.
A service endpoint associated with the DID subject.

states.definition: The agent identity has been created and registered but is not yet operating.
states.source: §2.9 SSO & Provisioning ('agent provisioning'); §3.7 ('from provisioning to ... de-provisioning')

states.definition: The agent has active credentials and an active session, operating in production.
states.source: Key Terms, 'Revocation' ('an agent's active credentials ... current session')

states.definition: The agent's active credentials are invalidated and its current session terminated; the underlying registration may still exist.
The formal and permanent removal of the agent's identity and all associated entitlements from all systems; the final step in its lifecycle.

grammar.$comment: Lifecycle state grammar (the state machine). Each key is a state; the array lists permitted next states. This MUST match fn::lifecycle_next_states in surreal/schema/agent_lifecycle.surql.

triggers.definition: A security alert event that initiates revocation or de-provisioning.
triggers.source: §3.2 ('triggered by an event like a security alert')

triggers.definition: The agent identity is suspected or known to be compromised.
triggers.source: §3.2 ('a compromise or end-of-life event'); 'De-provisioning is the ultimate response to a compromise')

triggers.definition: The agent has reached the end of its operational life and is decommissioned.

attributes.definition: The accountable owners of the agent identity.
attributes.source: §2.9 ('its own attributes, owners, and group memberships')

attributes.definition: The access rights associated with the agent identity.
attributes.source: §2.9 ('all its associated entitlements are irrevocably purged'); §3.2

attributes.definition: The acting party (agent) to whom authority has been delegated.
attributes.source: §2.11 ('the act (actor) claim ... identify the acting party')

attributes.definition: The human principal who granted authority.
attributes.source: §2.11 ('distinct identifiers for both the human principal and the agent actor')
